questions.question: Write and explain the basic structure of a C++ program with an example.
keyPoints: Preprocessor directives (#include) are used to include header files
main() function is the entry point of the program
Function body contains statements enclosed in braces {}

questions.question: Explain the difference between cin and cout with examples.
keyPoints: cout is used for output, cin is used for input

questions.question: What are comments in C++? Explain the two types with examples.
keyPoints: Comments are non-executable statements for documentation
Example: // This is a single-line comment

Example: /* This is a
multi-line comment */

questions.question: Explain the purpose of 'using namespace std;' in C++ programs.
keyPoints: using namespace std allows direct access to std members
Without it, must use std::cout, std::cin
Can be avoided by using std:: prefix or limiting scope

questions.question: What is the difference between endl and '\n' in C++?
keyPoints: endl also flushes the output buffer
Use endl when immediate display is needed

questions.question: Explain the role of preprocessor directives in C++ with example.
What is the purpose of the return statement in main()?
keyPoints: Returns control to the operating system
Return value is used by batch scripts/shell

questions.question: Explain the complete general form of a C++ program. Discuss each component with its purpose and include a well-commented sample program demonstrating all concepts.
keyPoints: Preprocessor directives (#include) - for including header files
Namespace declarations (using namespace std) - for avoiding name conflicts
main() function - entry point with return type int
Opening and closing braces - define function body
Complete sample program with all elements explained

questions.question: Write a C++ program to read two integers from the user and display their sum. Explain each line of the program with respect to the general form of C++ program.
keyPoints: Program structure follows standard C++ form
cin for reading input from user

questions.question: Discuss input and output operations in C++ using cin and cout. Include a program that demonstrates reading a string and an integer, then outputting them with proper formatting.
keyPoints: cin is the standard input stream object
cout is the standard output stream object
Sample program with string and integer I/O
Explanation of extraction and insertion operations

questions.question: Explain the importance of comments in C++ programs. Write a program demonstrating both single-line and multi-line comments with proper documentation of the code.
keyPoints: Comments are for human readers, ignored by compiler
